text.heading: Five-month old among 275 beneficiaries of free eye surgery
text.p: No fewer than 275 eye patients have been treated by the Indian doctors who visited Ogun State on a Rotary International Exchange programme.

A five-month-old child was among the patients that benefited from the medical programme which was at the instance of Rotary International District 9110 in partnership with the Indian community in Lagos and Ogun States.

Speaking at the "Save Our sight project 2007", Ogun State Health Commissioner, Dr. Abiodun Oduwole, pleaded with individuals and corporate bodies to help in providing health facilities to support Daniel administration in its effort to improve healthcare.
Oduwole said at the General Hospital, Ijaiye, Abeokuta, that doing so would help take the healthcare delivery beyond the present level, thus impacting positively in the lives of the citizens.
The leader of the 12-man team from India, Viral Purohit of Rotary Eye Institute, expressed the delight of the delegation in coming to Ogun State.
He said that the State had good medical programme, the development which informed the decision of the Indian doctors to visit it.
According to Purohit, who was accompanied by Rotarian D.S. Mirani, the team began the eye operation from Kaduna, before moving to Lagos and finally ended the trip in Ogun State.

"We cannot afford not to come to Ogun State because of the healthcare programme of the government here, coupled with the friendly disposition of the doctors and medical staff in this hospital and the people themselves," the doctor said.
He explained that among the eye diseases treated were cataract, sight partially lost by acid or fire burns, glaucoma and pterygium.
Purohit assured that the team would continue to offer necessary assistance to eye patients on yearly basis.
